Clayton Jones

Scenario 1: Data Collection and Summarization

Scenario 2: Clustering NWSL PLayers

Recreate Figure 8.3 using the updated dataset. To receive full credit, include the year in the title, provide labels for any scorer that is under 20, or has an xGp90 over 0.5. Include the player’s name and xGp90 value in the label.

## Warning: Removed 5 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).
## Warning: Removed 5 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_text_repel()`).
## Warning: ggrepel: 18 unlabeled data points (too many overlaps). Consider
## increasing max.overlaps

Note: There were so many names clustered in the bottom left corner that some were cut out automatically (15 total) when I initially ran the code. I tried to make the text smaller but it was still cutting a few out so I also tried to increase the max.overlaps, but it made the chart unreadable. I ended up decreasing the font size to 2 (default and reducing to 5 (default 5) to make the chart most readable, which excluded 19 labels. The NWSL must have added a bunch of younger players since 2022, as this was not a problem with the plot in the textbook.

Recreate Figure 8.10 using the updated dataset. Cluster the athletes using xGp90, xAp90 ensuring that you have appropriately normalized these values. Include the cluster (color) and position (shape) in the plot. In addition, provide a table that summarizes the number of athletes assigned to each cluster and position combination (a total of 9 possibilities).

Count of Each Position/Cluster Combination
Position Cluster Count
D 1 8
D 2 112
D 3 1
F 1 64
F 2 22
F 3 30
M 1 25
M 2 77
M 3 5

In the graph above, expected goals and assists are normalized to 90 minutes to account for different players playing different number of minutes. Looking at the Figure 2, cluster 2 is all low expected assists and goals per 90. Cluster 1 has higher expected goals per 90, and it is interesting that all players with expected assists per 90 greater than 0.3 were assigned to cluster 1. Cluster 3 is made up of the players with the highest expected goals per 90. It really seems as though it was mostly clustered by expected goals per 90 and not expected assists per 90.

It is interesting to see the spread of clusters fo each position in Table 1. 111 of 121 total defenders were assigned to cluster 1, which would make sense because defenders score and assist the least. Midfielders were primarily assigned to cluster 2, the cluster with the middle class of expected goals per 90 but also the most expected assists per 90. Forwards were spread almsot evenly among the clusters.

Recreate Figure 8.11 using the updated dataset. Cluster the athletes using player, squad, age, starts, xGp90, xAp90, xGxAp90. Next, provide a table that summarizes the number of players assigned to each cluster for k = 5, and k = 4, respectively. Lastly, provide a summary of which clusters (k = 5 and k = 4 previously) overlap, and which cluster (for k = 5) saw a decrease in assignment (for k = 4).

Number of Players Assigned to Each Cluster for k=5 and k=4
Cluster Count (k=4) Count (k=5)
1 117 117
2 208 101
3 13 107
4 6 13
5 NA 6

Table 2 shows the number of players assigned to each cluster for k=4 and k=5. The counts for clusters 1, 3, and 4 are same, meaning that those clusters overlap from k=4 and k=5. When going from 4 clusters to 5 clusters, cluster 2 is the one that split, and saw a decrease in assignment, going from 128 to 91. The difference between the two (57) is what makes up cluster 5 for k=5.

Scenario 3: Evaluating EPL Goal Keepers

Recreate the results for k-means clustering for k = 2 and k = 3, similar to Section 8.9 Step 1. Provide a table that summarizes the cluster means for k = 2 and k = 3, and provide a summary of the % of variation explained.

Cluster Means for k=2 and k=3
Cluster 1 Mean (k=2) Cluster 2 Mean (k=2) Cluster 1 Mean (k=3) Cluster 2 Mean (k=3) Cluster 3 Mean (k=3)
Postshot Expected Goals per Shot on Target 0.2913636 0.2661111 0.2387500 0.2961538 0.2863158
Launch Completion Percentage 34.03636 32.71667 31.63750 35.17692 33.01579
Pass Launch Percentage 24.06818 52.37778 64.31250 19.29231 37.21053
Average Pass Length 28.61818 39.44444 43.75000 26.54615 33.92105
Goal Kick Launch Percentage 42.89091 66.88333 72.41250 34.11538 59.19474
Defensive Actions Outside the Penalty Area per 90 Minutes 1.245455 1.452778 1.730000 1.373077 1.150526 
## between_SS / total_SS for k=2: 44.86% 
## between_SS / total_SS for k=3: 60.42%

As printed above, k=2 had a between_SS/total_SS value of 44.86%, whereas k=3 had a value of 60.42%. These values are the amount of variance in the dataset that can be explained by clustering. The higher value for k=3 means that the points are closer to the cluster means, and there is lower variance in each cluster, this making k=3 the superior choice for clustering this data.
